title: apium graveolens subsp. rapaceum
intro: Apium graveolens subsp. rapaceum, also known as turnip-rooted celery or wild celery, is a subspecies of the Apium graveolens plant, which is a biennial or perennial herb native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in its seeds, which contain apiole and other volatile oils with antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative effects. The health benefits of Apium graveolens subsp. rapaceum include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate menstrual cramps, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Apium graveolens subsp. rapaceum is commonly prepared as an essential oil, tea, or tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
